ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03315091 covers curry puff as a filled fried pastry; mutton-specific variant not separately coded — exact match at category level.
ontology.foodex2_description: Filled pastry, fried — curry puff type
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers fried filled pastry; no mutton-specific curry puff code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for curry puff or equivalent fried filled pastry snack identified in SNOMED CT International Edition as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard curry puff (mutton) piece weighs approximately 65 g and is typically consumed as a snack or light meal accompaniment.

nutrient_source.title: Curry Puff (Mutton) — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Curry Puff (Mutton), food code F11090009. All core macros and micronutrients sourced directly from this entry. Saturated fat estimated at 7.0 g/100g based on typical deep-fried mutton pastry composition cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fried meat-filled pastry; sugar estimated at 1.5 g/100g; potassium estimated at 190 mg/100g from USDA analogous data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Wheat-Based Fried Pastry Products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for wheat-flour fried pastry and potato-filled snacks
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for mutton curry puff exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 68 (medium) is estimated from the closest analogous foods: wheat-flour fried pastry shell (GI ~70) and potato-filled snack items (GI ~65–72) listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (68 × 22.8 g) / 100 ≈ 15.5. Available carbohydrate per 65 g serving = 35.1 × 0.65 = 22.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 575 mg/100g with high saturated fat 7.0 g/100g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit curry puff (mutton) to one piece maximum per occasion due to its high sodium content of 575 mg/100g, which contributes approximately 331 mg sodium per standard 65 g serving, and should avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods or condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate 35.1 g/100g with medium GI 68 and unfavourable carbohydrate-to-fibre ratio
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume curry puff (mutton) sparingly — no more than one small piece — as the refined wheat pastry shell and potato filling deliver a medium GI of 68 with 35.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g and a glycemic load of approximately 15.5 per standard serving, which can cause meaningful postprandial glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 7.0 g/100g, trans fat 0.2 g/100g, sodium 575 mg/100g, and deep-fried preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit curry puff (mutton) due to its combination of saturated fat (7.0 g/100g), trans fat (0.2 g/100g from pastry shortening), high sodium (575 mg/100g), and deep-fried preparation, all of which contribute to atherogenic risk and elevated blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mutton (red meat) high in arachidonic acid, trans fat 0.2 g/100g, deep-frying generating AGEs, and potato as nightshade vegetable
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid curry puff (mutton) as mutton is a red meat rich in arachidonic acid that amplifies inflammatory prostaglandin pathways, trans fat from pastry shortening is directly pro-inflammatory, deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation, and potato is a nightshade vegetable that may trigger flares in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Curry Puff (Mutton) is a deep-fried savoury pastry snack popular in Singapore's mamak food culture, consisting of a flaky, crispy wheat-flour pastry shell encasing a spiced filling of minced or diced mutton (lamb), cubed potato, hard-boiled egg, and aromatic curry paste made with turmeric, cumin, coriander, and chilli. The pastry is characteristically crimped at the edges and deep-fried in oil until golden brown, producing a crunchy exterior with a moist, fragrant, mildly spiced interior. Per 100 g, it provides 305 kcal, 5.6 g protein, 15.8 g fat (including 7.0 g saturated fat and 0.2 g trans fat), 35.1 g carbohydrate, 3.2 g dietary fibre, and 575 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 68 (medium), driven by the refined wheat pastry shell and potato filling. Clinically, the high sodium content (575 mg/100g) is a concern for hypertension management; the medium-GI refined carbohydrate load is unfavourable for Type 2 Diabetes glycaemic control; and the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and deep-frying raises cardiovascular disease risk. The mutton filling also makes this unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to arachidonic acid content. It is halal-certified and a staple of mamak stalls and kopitiams across Singapore, commonly consumed as a morning or afternoon snack.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a curry puff?
Is curry puff high in sodium?
Mutton curry puff calories per piece

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (food code F11090009) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, sugar, potassium, and cholesterol estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous fried meat-filled pastry data and documented accordingly. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous wheat-flour fried pastry entries.
notes: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed vitamin, mineral, and moisture data for this specific mamak-style mutton curry puff preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. Saturated fat estimated at 7.0 g/100g, monounsaturated fat at 5.5 g/100g, polyunsaturated fat at 2.1 g/100g, trans fat at 0.2 g/100g, sugar at 1.5 g/100g, potassium at 190 mg/100g, and cholesterol at 28.0 mg/100g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous fried meat-filled pastry composition data. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat-flour fried pastry and potato-filled snack entries; no direct measurement exists for mutton curry puff. The dietary_tag 'high_fibre' from the input has been removed as 3.2 g/100g dietary fibre does not meet the Singapore HPB high-fibre threshold of ≥6 g/100g for a high-fibre claim. Halal dietary flag retained as mamak-style mutton curry puff is prepared with halal-certified mutton.
